description: Add optional Telerik NuGet credential support to the Ralph sandbox, following the same pattern as the GitHub PAT

userStories.title: Read Telerik password from environment variable
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the sandbox script to read TELERIK_PASSWORD from the environment so I can provide Telerik NuGet credentials without interactive prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: Script reads TELERIK_PASSWORD environment variable
When TELERIK_PASSWORD is set, logs 'Using TELERIK_PASSWORD from environment'
When TELERIK_PASSWORD is not set, no prompt is shown and no error or warning is logged
Username is hardcoded to 'api-key'
Build passes
userStories.notes: Mirror the $GitHubPat pattern in ralph-sandbox.ps1 lines 306-317, but without the interactive fallback prompt — just skip silently if not set.

userStories.title: Include Telerik feed in generated nuget.config when credentials are provided
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the generated nuget.config to include the Telerik NuGet feed and credentials when TELERIK_PASSWORD is set, so dotnet restore can pull Telerik packages inside the sandbox.
acceptanceCriteria: New-NuGetConfig accepts an optional TelerikPassword parameter
When TelerikPassword is provided, nuget.config includes package source for https://nuget.telerik.com/v3/index.json with key 'Telerik'
Credentials section includes username 'api-key' and ClearTextPassword from the parameter
Package source mapping includes '<package pattern="Telerik.*" />' for the Telerik source
When TelerikPassword is NOT provided, nuget.config output is identical to current behavior
Build passes
userStories.notes: The New-NuGetConfig function at line ~34 of ralph-sandbox.ps1 uses a here-string template. Conditionally build the Telerik XML sections only when the password is provided.

userStories.title: Pass Telerik password through to nuget.config generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the main script flow to wire the TELERIK_PASSWORD value into the New-NuGetConfig call so the credential is embedded in the container's nuget.config.
acceptanceCriteria: The New-NuGetConfig call at ~line 320 passes TelerikPassword when available
No changes needed to the Dockerfile — existing nuget.config copy mechanism handles this
No changes needed to network policy — nuget.telerik.com is already in the allow-list
Staged nuget.config cleanup in the finally block still works correctly
Build passes
userStories.notes: Small wiring change — just pass the $TelerikPassword variable to the New-NuGetConfig call alongside $GitHubPat.

userStories.title: Add upfront prompt asking if user needs Telerik NuGet feed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the script to ask me at the beginning whether I need Telerik NuGet support, so the Telerik source is only added when actually needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Script prompts user early (before sandbox creation) whether they need Telerik NuGet feed
If user says no, Telerik is completely skipped (no env check, no credential prompt, no nuget.config changes)
If user says yes, proceed with Telerik credential resolution (US-005)
Prompt is clear and defaults to 'No' if user just presses Enter
Build passes
userStories.notes: PR comment by @vas-pashkov-freemarket: Should we ask the user in the beginning of the script if they need Telerik and only add it if they need it?

userStories.title: Add interactive fallback prompt for Telerik password
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the script to prompt me for my Telerik password if it's not set as an environment variable, following the same pattern as the GitHub PAT.
acceptanceCriteria: When user opted in to Telerik (US-004) and TELERIK_PASSWORD env var is not set, script prompts user to enter the password interactively
Interactive prompt follows the same pattern as the Git PAT prompt (Read-Host)
If user provides a password interactively, it is used for nuget.config generation
If user skips/cancels the prompt, Telerik is skipped gracefully
Build passes
userStories.notes: PR comment by @vas-pashkov-freemarket: here we should follow the pattern for the Git PAT, if its not set on env level we should ask the user to provide the password
